pages:
Calderwood Partners LLCwww.calderwoodpartners.com
June 24, 2009
Steven Lowe
Vice President, Finance
Key Group
Re: Growth Strategy for Key Group
Dear Mr. Lowe:
This letter sets the terms on which we will help Key Group decide where its growth is going to come from
over the next several years and what it will take to capture it. It records the work, the people who will do it,
the fee, and the conditions we work under. Please read it against your own memory of our conversations. If
any line here does not match what you understood, telephone me before you sign, so we settle it at the
outset. It is written to be read alongside your own recollection of our recent conversations, and it is
deliberately specific about what we will and will not do, because an engagement of this kind depends on
both sides holding the same understanding of scope from the first day. Once you countersign, this describes
the scope, and we will hold to it.
The Work
Our task is to answer a single question and answer it well: where should Key Group look for its next
several years of growth, and what will it cost to get there. Within that, the engagement does four things. We
will read the current position, establishing where the company earns its money today by segment and by
customer, and where that base is growing or thinning. We will size the market, measuring the segments the
company serves and the near ones it does not, and marking where demand is moving. We will frame the
growth options, covering deeper penetration of the core, extension into adjacent segments, and geographic
extension, each carrying an honest account of what it would require and what it would return. And we will
prioritize, ranking the options on return and on what the company can realistically execute, and laying out a
plan for the ones worth pursuing.
What This Engagement Is Not
The boundaries are worth stating plainly, so that none of them is left to inference. This is not a valuation,
and we take no view on the price of any transaction. We do not raise capital or advise on financing. We do
not provide legal or tax advice; where a question turns on either, we will say so in writing and refer you to
Calderwood Partners LLC
Page 1 of 3

your own counsel. Our work ends at a recommendation and a plan you can act on. We do not implement it
for you, though we are glad to be asked separately if you want that help later.
How We Will Work
We build from your own numbers, not from a template, and we document as we go, so any figure that
reaches a deliverable can be tied back to a source your finance staff already recognize. We keep findings
separate from recommendations and label each for what it is, because the two carry different weight. And
we bring interim results to you as they firm up, so nothing in the final material arrives as a surprise.
Who Will Do It
Brenda Rodriguez, Senior Consultant, will lead the analysis day to day and will be your team's first point of
contact on any matter of substance. Daniel Leach, Research Associate, will run the market sizing and the
data collection, and your finance staff will hear from him most often. I read every deliverable before it
leaves this office. For scheduling and data requests, our contact on your side will be you, Steven Lowe,
unless you direct us to someone else.
Fee and Start Date
Our fee to Key Group for the work described above is $113,000, fixed for that scope. We will begin on July
4, 2009, and we plan on approximately seven months from that date to the final material. If you ask us to
widen the scope, we will price the addition in writing and will not begin it until you have agreed the price.
Invoicing
We will invoice the fixed fee in equal monthly installments across the term, each payable within thirty days
of its date. Out-of-pocket expenses, principally travel to your sites, are billed at cost and itemized on the
invoice. We do not mark expenses up.
Term and Termination
The engagement runs from the start date set out above until we deliver the final material, unless either of us
ends it sooner. Either party may terminate on fifteen days' written notice, for any reason or none. On
termination you will owe the fee for work performed to the effective date, prorated against the fixed fee,
together with expenses incurred, and we will hand over the work product in whatever state it has then
reached.
Confidentiality
We will treat the information you give us as confidential, use it only for this engagement, and disclose it
outside our firm only as you direct in writing or as law requires. Growth work of this kind touches plans
you have not yet made public, and we will handle it with that in mind. We ask the same of you as to our
methods, our workpapers, and the terms of this fee.
Calderwood Partners LLC
Page 2 of 3

Our Liability
Our total liability arising out of this engagement, on any theory, is limited to the fees actually paid to us
under it. Neither party will be liable to the other for indirect, incidental, or consequential loss, including lost
profits, however it arises. This limit does not reach our own fraud or willful misconduct. Our deliverables
are prepared for your internal use; if you intend to give one to a third party who may rely on it, raise it with
us first so that we can address the reliance in writing.
Governing Law
This letter is governed by the law of the state in which our principal office sits, without regard to its
conflict-of-laws rules. Any dispute we cannot resolve between us will go to mediation before either party
commences litigation.
If these terms are acceptable, please sign both copies of this letter and return one to us; the other is yours to
keep. We are glad to be doing this work for Key Group, and we will be ready to begin on the date set out
above.
Sincerely,
Julie Ramsey
Managing Principal
Date: June 24, 2009
Steven Lowe
VP Finance, Key Group
Date: June 24, 2009
Calderwood Partners LLC
Page 3 of 3
